info.title: Watch Schedule API
info.description: HTTP API for reading the crew watch schedule and starting/stopping the watch managed by the signalk-watch-schedule plugin.

tags.name: state
tags.description: Current watch state and resolved schedule

tags.name: config
tags.description: Teams, defaults, and available watch systems

WatchState.description: The plugin's authoritative runtime state. Everything else (system, schedule, current/next shift) is derived from this plus the plugin configuration.

onWatch.description: Whether the boat is currently standing watches.

startedAt.description: Epoch milliseconds the current watch began, always snapped to a whole local clock hour. Null when no watch is running.

systemId.description: Id of the active watch rotation system (e.g. `fixed-4-4`). Null when no watch is running.

teamOrder.description: Permutation of the configured teams' indices chosen for this watch — the team at position 0 is first on watch. Null means the natural configuration order.

name.description: Team or crew member name, e.g. "Port Watch".

offset.description: Start of the segment in minutes from the cycle anchor (the watch start, or local midnight for clock-anchored systems).

duration.description: Length of the segment in minutes.

teamIndex.description: 0-based index into the ordered team list of the team standing this segment.

label.description: Traditional watch name (e.g. "First Dog"). Only present on systems that define labels.

WatchSystem.description: A time-agnostic rotation definition: segments as minute offsets and durations, repeated every cycleDuration minutes.

teamCount.description: Exact number of teams this rotation requires.

cycleDuration.description: Length of one full rotation cycle in minutes.

anchored.description: True when segment offsets are read from local midnight (watches land on fixed clock hours) rather than from the watch start.

ResolvedShift.description: A concrete shift: a system segment resolved to absolute times and an actual team.

teamIndex.description: 0-based index into the ordered team list.

color.description: Stable hex color assigned to the team, e.g. `#dc2626`.

label.description: Traditional watch name, when the system defines one.

isCurrent.description: True for the shift on duty right now.

cycleIndex.description: How many full rotation cycles have elapsed at this shift.

WatchData.description: The full watch view — the same data the plugin publishes under the SignalK `watch.*` paths. When no watch is running, `system`, `current`, and `next` are null and `schedule` is empty.

system.description: The active watch system, or null when idle.

teams.description: Effective teams in watch order (the per-watch teamOrder already applied): position 0 is first on watch.

current.description: The shift on duty right now, or null.

next.description: The next shift coming on duty, or null.

schedule.description: Ordered upcoming shifts, starting with the current one. Length is the configured publish horizon.

teams.description: Effective teams in configuration order, after the config → communication.crewNames → default fallback.

defaultSystemId.description: Rotation pre-selected when starting a watch without an explicit systemId.

publishHorizon.description: How many upcoming shifts are published and returned in `schedule`.

Unauthorized.description: Server security is enabled and the request carries no authenticated SignalK user.

get.summary: Get the current watch state and resolved schedule
get.description: Returns the authoritative state plus everything derived from it: the active system, the ordered teams, the current and next shift, and the upcoming schedule. This is the same view published under the SignalK `watch.*` paths.
Returns the effective watch teams and the UI-relevant plugin defaults.
Returns the built-in rotation systems that require exactly the number of teams currently configured. Only these ids are accepted by `/watch/start`.

post.description: Starts a watch with the requested rotation system. The start time is snapped to a whole local clock hour using the configured rounding mode and defaults to now; a team order may be supplied to pick who is first on watch. Calling this while a watch is already running restarts it with the new parameters. Requires authentication when server security is enabled.

systemId.description: Id of the watch system to run (see GET /systems). Defaults to the configured default system.

startAt.description: Requested start time, epoch milliseconds — snapped to a whole local clock hour. May be in the past (to sync a watch that already began) or the future. Defaults to now.

teamOrder.description: Permutation of the configured teams' indices; the team at position 0 is first on watch. Ignored unless it is a valid permutation. Defaults to configuration order.

400.description: Unknown watch system, or one unavailable for the configured team count.

example.error: unknown or unavailable watch system: fixed-9-9

post.description: Stops the running watch and clears the per-watch team order. Idempotent — succeeds whether or not a watch is running. Requires authentication when server security is enabled.
